page: Google Cloud Platform c4n-highmem-8 is an x86_64 architecture server within the c4n family. It features 8 dedicated vCPUs and 62.0 GB of system memory, providing a resource density of 7.75 GB of RAM per vCPU. This high memory-to-core ratio positions the instance for workloads requiring substantial memory capacity relative to compute resources.

The instance does not include local storage, meaning all storage requirements must be met using external block storage options. It contains no GPU accelerators and does not include complimentary public IPv4 addresses. The dedicated CPU allocation ensures consistent processing performance without resource contention from neighboring instances.

This hardware profile is qualitatively cost-efficient for memory-bound applications that do not require local NVMe storage or hardware acceleration. It is suited for workloads such as in-memory databases, caching layers, and enterprise application servers.

With its dedicated CPU allocation, it provides predictable performance for workloads such as in-memory databases, caching layers, and enterprise applications.
